name: Source Fidelity review for Transposon And Somatic Mutation Control bundle
summary: The staged source, study, intervention, and finding records match the cited PubMed and registry records closely enough for publication review. The LINE1/SIRT6, De Cecco lamivudine, 3TC mouse cognition, tauopathy mouse, ART-AD, CANTOS/TET2, CANTOS malignancy, and TIMI CHIP claims preserve source boundaries, endpoint type, sample size where material, and major caveats.

findings.claim_or_issue: The registry lists NCT04552795 as phase 1/2, while the publication describes ART-AD as a phase IIa open-label trial; the staged study uses the repo enum value phase2.
findings.why_it_matters: This is a minor metadata compression caused by the schema enum, not a substantive source-fidelity failure.
findings.recommended_action: No revision required; keep the public wording focused on the 24-week open-label phase IIa publication and preserve the registry ID for traceability.

findings.claim_or_issue: The CANTOS/TET2 source supports exploratory cardiovascular outcome association with canakinumab in TET2 CHIP patients, but it does not show reduction of mutation burden or clone size.
findings.why_it_matters: The staged finding and outlook correctly frame this as downstream IL-1beta modulation, so the boundary is preserved.
findings.recommended_action: No revision required; retain the explicit downstream-inflammatory caveat during editorial approval.
